title: Equine parietal bone harvesting
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c9drb","text":"This is the procedure carried out to etract beam-like specimens from the parietal bone of horse skull.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ddd0v","text":"The general procedure is divided in different phases:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2mk4g","text":"- Braincase extraction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"51n5n","text":"- Sample cutting","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4h1b6","text":"- Sample storage","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"4ovd4","text":"- At least two people should be involved in the first part of the process.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"903c0","text":"- The horse skull(s) should be thawed at 4°C for at least 24 hours before the procedure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"e0et8","text":"Throughout the whole process you should wear:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4u031","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eiba3","text":"- Latex gloves or similar","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3gkhq","text":"- Safety goggles","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2rti8","text":"- Lab coat","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"evo3d","text":"- Disposable apron","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5jtek","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"a3ola","text":"Vertical band saw, scalpel, ruler, pencil, saline solution 90%, steril gauze.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: In a room at 22°C: - Using a scalpel, carefully remove soft tissue removed from the bone. - Remove the soft organs attached to the inner surface of the bone (brain, cerebellum, dura mater, etc.). - Permanently irrigate the bone with saline solution.

Horse Braincase (left) and Sample template drawing in the parietal bone (right) - With the pencil, draw a square upon the parietal bone of the skull. The dimensions should cover the surface of at least one rectangular sample: 52 mm * 12 mm (taking into account the thickness of the saw). Note: alignment of the sample template should be done according to the direction to test. - The rectangle size will change according to the number of samples to cut.

- Align the skull so the vertical band saw is perpendicular to the surface to cut. - Carefully cut the individual sample following the pattern previously drawn, letting the saw go all the way through the thickness. - Permanently irrigate the bone with saline solution
- Wrap individual samples in gauze sodden with saline solution. - Store at 4°C.

Cutting of the brain case from a horse head In a room at 22°C: - Carefully place the horse head in the vertical band saw - Cut the section that encloses the braincase: from the occipital bone to the beginning of the frontal bone. - Permanently irrigate the bone with saline solution. At least two people should be involved in this part of the process
creator: Lilibeth A L.A.Z.M. Zambrano M
